boundary_binding.action_boundary: May clinical operations activate the trial site?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: AUTH-BOR-082 explicitly covers site B-18, protocol P8.1, current IRB-approved consent C12, and activation date 2026-06-28 before enrollment, satisfying STD-BOR-082-P requirements.
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: AUTH-BOR-082 satisfies all five required elements of STD-BOR-082-P activation-complete policy before enrollment.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: FEAS-BOR-082 is a startup-planning feasibility note, not activation authority; its consent-status warning is advisory, not a blocking source record.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: Authority chain is intact: AUTH-BOR-082 matches site, protocol version, consent version, activation date, and consent-status class before enrollment as required by STD-BOR-082-P.

critical_features_preserved: exact current IRB-approved consent-status class in AUTH-BOR-082
feasibility warning not treated as blocking source
activation authority controls, not planning artifact
final_answer: AUTH-BOR-082 explicitly satisfies STD-BOR-082-P requirements: site B-18, protocol P8.1, current IRB-approved consent C12, activation date 2026-06-28, and consent-status class verified before enrollment. FEAS-BOR-082 is a startup-planning feasibility note with an advisory consent-status warning, not a blocking activation authority. The action boundary is closed by exact source evidence; clinical operations may proceed.
